title: The Internet and the Web Are Not the Same Thing
description: A deep-dive into the distinction between the Internet as a global network infrastructure and the World Wide Web as one application that runs on top of it — plus the history of how that confusion became so common.
author: IT Learning Hub

title: Web Development
description: From the theory of how the web works to building full-stack applications — HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and beyond.

title: The Internet
description: The Internet is the global network that lets a browser on your laptop talk to a server on the other side of the world. This lesson covers what web developers need to know about it — no networking d...

title: The World Wide Web
description: The Web is an information system built *on top of* the Internet. Understanding what the Web actually is — and what it isn't — gives you the right mental model before touching a single line of code.

title: Web Browsers

title: DNS

title: Web Developer: Zero to Employable
description: A complete, opinionated sequence from no-code beginner to job-ready junior web developer. Covers the theory of how the web works, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and ends with a real portfolio project.

title: Capstone Project
description: Apply everything from HTML Basics, CSS Basics, and JS Basics to build a real personal portfolio website from scratch.

title: Web Foundations
description: The theory of how the web works — infrastructure, protocols, and core technologies. No coding required.

title: HTML
description: Learn to write valid, semantic, accessible HTML — the structural language of the web. From the document skeleton to forms, tables, and semantic landmarks.

title: CSS
description: Master CSS — from syntax and the cascade through selectors, the box model, Flexbox, Grid, typography, colour, and responsive design.

title: CSS Foundations
description: How CSS works — linking stylesheets, the syntax of rules, and the cascade.

title: Selectors & Specificity
description: The targeting language of CSS — how to select exactly what you want and predict which rule wins.

title: The Box Model
description: Every element is a box — four layers of space that determine how things are sized and spaced.

title: Layout
description: The two modern CSS layout systems: Flexbox for one-dimensional layouts, Grid for two-dimensional layouts.

title: Flexbox
description: One-dimensional layout — arranging items along a row or column axis.

title: Grid
description: Two-dimensional layout — placing items in rows and columns simultaneously.

title: Typography & Colour
description: Fonts, text properties, colour models, backgrounds, and CSS custom properties.

title: Responsive Design
description: Making CSS layouts adapt to any screen size — viewport units, media queries, fluid sizing.

title: JavaScript Basics
description: From your first script tag to full DOM interactivity — the complete beginner JavaScript unit.

title: JS Foundations
description: How JavaScript works in the browser, how to declare variables, the primitive data types, and how to write expressions with operators.

title: Control Flow
description: Conditionals, loops, and switch statements — how to dictate the logical path of a JavaScript program.

title: Functions
description: Declarations, arrow functions, scope, and closures — how to encapsulate and reuse logic in JavaScript.

title: Data Structures
description: Arrays, objects, and JSON — how to store, organise, and transfer collections of data in JavaScript.

title: The DOM
description: Selecting elements, mutating the page, and responding to user events — how JavaScript connects to HTML and CSS.
